fields.label: Final calculation of liquid bulk delivered
Was tank cleaning completed at the end of the previous charter?
Has an initial manual sounding/ullage of the shore tank been carried out by the surveyor using a calibrated sounding tape?
Has the increased loading rate been agreed between ship and shore, and the shore loading lines and connections inspected immediately on ramp-up?
Shore loading line inspected and found satisfactory?
Have the lines been blown through and verified after completion of discharge?
Did the previous charterer's tank cleaning procedure include a line displacement with brine?
Has an initial photograph of the shore flow meter been taken?
Has the cargo line been disconnected from the ship's manifold prior to commencement of final soundings/ullages?
Is this the first loadout of liquid bulk cargo under the current charter?
Is the shipper's shore flow meter calibration certificate up to date and calibrated for the cargo's specific gravity?
Was a line sample taken at the ship's manifold on resumption of loading?
Has a final manual sounding/ullage of the ship's tanks been carried out by the surveyor using a calibrated sounding tape?
Tank nomination & this loadout's cargo history
Have the shore loading lines been inspected, serial numbers verified against certificates, and pressure tested within the last 12 months?
Has a final photograph of the ship's flow meter been taken?
Was the 2nd line sample visually inspected and approved?
What was the previous cargo loaded in the tanks?
What is the volume from the ship's flow meter?
Has the cargo certificate been issued and signed by the vessel, surveyor and shipper representatives?
Was tank cleaning carried out prior to loading this cargo?
Have the height(s) of the vessel's sounding tube(s) been verified?
Were periodic samples taken at the ship's manifold during loading?
Did that tank cleaning include a line displacement with brine?
Has an initial manual sounding/ullage of the ship's tanks been carried out by the surveyor using a calibrated sounding tape?
Has a final manual sounding/ullage of the shore tank been carried out by the surveyor using a calibrated sounding tape?
Were the periodic samples visually inspected and found satisfactory?
Which cargo tanks are nominated to load this cargo?
Has an initial photograph of the ship's flow meter been taken?
Has a final photograph of the shipper's shore flow meter been taken?
Is the ship's flow meter calibration certificate up to date and calibrated for the cargo's specific gravity?
What is the volume from the shipper's shore flow meter?
Is more than one cargo type being loaded?
Were the vessel loading lines inspected via borescope and found fit to load cargo?
Are segregated loading lines, pumps and manifolds (including discharge lines) being used for each product type?
Has cross-contamination been evaluated by the shipper and advised to the charterer?
Has the transfer rate been agreed between ship and shore, including the initial slow start-up procedure?
Has the tank loading sequence been agreed, with each tank to load an initial first foot individually?
Have the above questions been notified to charterers prior to loading?
Was a line sample taken at the ship's manifold at commencement of loading?
Are spill kits (including containment booms) available and ready to deploy on ship and shore for oil-based cargoes?
Was the line sample visually inspected and approved?
Was a first foot sample taken from each tank?
Were the first foot samples visually inspected and approved?
Have the charterers been notified that the cargo is off-spec?
